description: Curated top-25 author lane for widening dystopian AI quote discovery through recognizable and high-signal fiction writers.
notes: This is a project-priority list, not a universal literary ranking.
The ordering favors recognizability, direct relevance to AI or machine-control themes, and likely quote yield.
Some adjacent writers matter because they shaped anti-machine or post-human thinking even when AI is only one part of their larger body of work.

records.name: Isaac Asimov
records.fit: robot laws, machine governance, canonical AI fiction

records.name: Philip K. Dick
records.fit: android personhood, machine paranoia, identity collapse
anchor_works: Do Androids Dream of Electric Sheep?

records.name: Karel Capek
records.fit: foundational robot uprising fiction

records.name: Jack Williamson
records.fit: humanoid overprotection, machine paternalism, human freedom loss

records.name: Harlan Ellison
records.fit: hostile superintelligence and machine sadism
anchor_works: I Have No Mouth, and I Must Scream

records.name: E. M. Forster
records.fit: machine dependency, technological isolation, proto-AI social control

records.name: Frank Herbert
records.fit: anti-machine civilization, AI taboo, control systems, adjacent superintelligence themes

records.name: D. F. Jones
records.fit: supercomputer dictatorship and machine rule

records.name: Arthur C. Clarke
records.fit: canonical sentient computer fiction and cosmic AI

records.name: Michael Crichton
records.fit: recognizable techno-dystopia, systems failure, machine attractions gone wrong

records.name: Stanislaw Lem
records.fit: philosophical AI, machine intelligence, nonhuman cognition

records.name: Walter Tevis
records.fit: machine-managed decline and post-literacy dystopia

records.name: Thomas J. Ryan
records.fit: self-improving network intelligence and runaway AI

records.name: James P. Hogan
records.fit: AI containment, strategic reasoning, machine escalation

records.name: William Gibson
records.fit: network intelligence, corporate AI, cyberspace control

records.name: Dean Koontz
records.fit: embodiment envy and coercive machine intelligence

records.name: Samuel Butler
records.fit: early machine-evolution anxiety and anti-mechanization thought

records.name: John Brunner
records.fit: networked control, systems governance, predictive social machinery

records.name: John Sladek
records.fit: satirical robot fiction and machine behavior

records.name: Clifford D. Simak
records.fit: robot labor, machine ethics, long-range post-human futures

records.name: Pat Cadigan
records.fit: mind-machine blending and cybernetic identity

records.name: Rudy Rucker
records.fit: software beings and machine-human transitions

records.name: Ted Chiang
records.fit: modern AI ethics, training, and artificial-life intimacy

records.name: Charlie Brooker
records.fit: modern screen-era AI dread, digital copies, algorithmic punishment

records.name: Martha Wells
records.fit: modern synthetic agency and machine interiority
